behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: real environment behavior (git branch/commit ancestry and PR presence together). The failure can hide if git history and PR state are mocked independently; real stale detection often depends on actual git refs/history traversal and command behavior. Pass 2 mapping: real subprocess/repo behavior => System. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is deterministic and contains no AI/ML judgment or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo with `main` and feature branches, add commits not in `main`, age them past threshold, and run the actual staleness detector with real PR-state lookup (or a real local PR-state source used by the app). Assert branch is classified stale only when all three predicates hold: ahead of main, older than threshold, and no open PR.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: module wiring (session bootstrap + detector + warning renderer). Failure boundary is handoff of computed stale state into emitted warning payload. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text itself; no AI output; no multi-step agent chain. Rejection gate: text says "session-start setup emits ... when the current branch is stale" and does not require validating real shell startup/event hooks, so System is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke session-start initialization in-process with a wired stale-state provider returning stale current branch metadata, and assert one warning event/message is emitted containing actionable context (branch name, age, and PR status/no-PR cue).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: pure local logic (deterministic mapping function). No module boundary or I/O is needed to expose failure. Pass 2 mapping: pure function => Unit. Rejected higher levels because behavior explicitly says "local status-classifier function" and "deterministically."
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive inputs of computed branch state objects into the classifier and assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) with repeat-run determinism checks.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: real environment/system behavior. Behavior explicitly requires "against live repo and PR state," so mocks can hide failures in command wiring, auth, API shape, repo state reads, and render integration. Pass 2 mapping: real HTTP/repo/subprocess interactions => System. Rejected higher levels because no AI/ML quality judgment or multi-agent pipeline is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in a real test repository with real PR-state source and assert rendered output transitions between `clean` and `STALE` for controlled branch scenarios (no extra commits vs commits-without-PR).

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local module interaction between staleness logic and PR-state interpretation (open/merged semantics). This can be exposed with realistic PR-state fixtures wired through real mapper modules. Integration brake: behavior text does not require live network/OS/subprocess, no AI dependence, no multi-step agent chain. Rejection gate: text only states outcome for "open or merged PRs" and does not require live API calls, so System is not mandatory.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector with real PR-state parsing/mapping modules and feed branch states linked to `open` and `merged` PR records; assert classifier/output never returns `stale-no-pr` for those cases.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: real git-history behavior under rebase/squash transformations. Mocked commit lists often miss ancestry/merge-base edge cases that occur with real rewritten history. Pass 2 mapping: requires real repo operations (and often real git subprocess behavior) => System. Rejected higher levels because this is deterministic source-control logic, not AI/ML or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo fixture, create feature work, then apply rebase and squash-merge flows that rewrite hashes; run staleness detection after each transformation and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale when work is effectively integrated.
